_doc: The provider/framework matrix as DATA — the api-doc->http-mcp mapping, not code. A host runner expands x_axes x frameworks into wire calls: physics picks the tool (http_request | bidi_command), endpoint+caps_template build the target, inject_auth says where the resolved profile credential goes BELOW the boundary. http-mcp stays 3 tools; this is specs/ data, the same discipline as the route priors. Verified live on 2026-06-24 via http_request/bidi_command + auth profile prod:adminltqa.

auth.resolve: auth profile (env LT_USERNAME/LT_ACCESS_KEY or gitignored auth/<profile>.json) — the secret never crosses the agent boundary

inject_auth_shapes.basic_header: WebDriver/Appium/HyperExecute over HTTP — Authorization: Basic base64(user:key)
inject_auth_shapes.caps_url: CDP/Puppeteer ws — user/accessKey injected into LT:Options inside ?capabilities=<urlencoded json>

selenium-bidi.note: session created over HTTP (CALL); webSocketUrl:true returns a BiDi ws (CHANNEL) for bidi_command. se:cdp gives raw CDP on the same session.

appium.verified: wire-CALL-appium-vd-validate — apk_arena (com.example.apk_arena) on Galaxy S22 emulator, passed

puppeteer.note: raw CDP — bidi_command drives it; timeout_ms >= 60000 for cold-start.

playwright.note: CATCH: /playwright speaks Playwright's OWN protocol (a CDP superset), NOT raw CDP. bidi_command cannot drive it — a host-side Playwright client (8/pilot) must. For the raw-CDP path use the puppeteer entry.

cypress.note: job submission — POST a HyperExecute YAML + repo, poll for results. Not a live remote-control.

xcuitest.note: native iOS — driven via WDA over HTTP (a CALL), or a native test job on HyperExecute.

espresso.note: native Android — submitted as a test job (a CALL).
